summary: Matched a group of previously stubbed Yoshi Egg Roll / SpecialS functions in ftYs_SpecialS.c, with supporting prototype and Yoshi struct/attribute layout updates. The PR replaces multiple placeholder comments with C implementations for callback setup, damage/death reset handling, ground and air SpecialS entry, start animation transitions, air loop physics, collision transitions, landing collision, and facing-direction reversal behavior. It also refines ftYoshi_FighterVars, ftYoshiAttributes, and ftYoshi_MotionVars.specials fields so the matched code can address real Vec3, velocity, timer, and tuning fields instead of broad padding.
what_changed: Added implementations for several previously unmatched ftYs_SpecialS.c functions, including fn_8012EC7C, fn_8012EDE8, fn_8012EFF4, ftYs_SpecialS_Enter, ftYs_SpecialAirS_Enter, ftYs_SpecialAirSStart_0_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSStart_1_Anim, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Phys, ftYs_SpecialAirSStart_0_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirSStart_1_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Coll, and fn_8013295C.
Introduced inline helper ftYoshi_SpecialS_SetCall to assign Yoshi SpecialS callbacks: death2_cb to fn_8012EC7C, take_dmg_cb to fn_8012EDE8, deal_dmg_cb to fn_8012EFF4, and x21F8 to fn_8013295C.
Introduced inline helper fn_8012EC7C_inline to share the reset/cleanup behavior used directly by fn_8012EC7C and after the hit effect/SFX path in fn_8012EDE8.
Updated SpecialS and SpecialHi headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes to concrete void functions taking Fighter_GObj* for fn_8012EC7C, fn_8012EDE8, fn_8012EFF4, and ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C.
Added declaration for fn_8013295C in ftYs_SpecialS.h.
Expanded ftYoshi_FighterVars.x222C from three u32 words to Vec3, matching its use as a JObj scale vector passed to HSD_JObjSetScale.
Expanded ftYoshiAttributes with named offsets used by the new SpecialS code, including x50, x5C, x7C, x8C, x90, xCC, and xE0, while preserving padding around them.
Expanded ftYoshi_MotionVars.specials with named fields xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, x20, x24, and x30, replacing a large anonymous padding range.
Added necessary includes for SpecialHi declarations, math constants, baselib forward declarations, ft_0877, ftanim, ftparts, effects, and lb_00B0.
Normalized some floating constants from 0.0f to 0.0F in nearby SpecialS code.
smart_moves: Factored repeated SpecialS callback assignment into ftYoshi_SpecialS_SetCall, making transition functions shorter and reducing the chance of inconsistent callback wiring.
Factored the reset path into fn_8012EC7C_inline so fn_8012EDE8 can spawn the damage effect/SFX and then reuse the same cleanup behavior as fn_8012EC7C.
Used struct layout discoveries to improve type accuracy rather than leaving code dependent on byte padding; notably x222C became Vec3 because it is passed by address to HSD_JObjSetScale.

Matched paired physics functions ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Phys and ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Phys with identical control-stick acceleration, speed clamping, and fall logic, preserving likely duplicated original behavior instead of over-abstracting it.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK calls, explicit motion-state IDs/flags, callback order, and ternary absolute-value patterns.
decomp_lessons: When a placeholder function’s callers all pass Fighter_GObj*, update headers away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS as soon as the signature is proven; this improves type checking across sibling headers.
Yoshi SpecialS code relies heavily on motion-variable offsets; naming individual fields in ftYoshi_MotionVars.specials can unlock multiple functions in one PR.
Attribute structs should be expanded only at offsets evidenced by code usage. This PR adds fields at specific offsets while retaining padding elsewhere, which keeps ABI layout stable.
Repeated transition boilerplate in fighter action code can often reveal small callback setup helpers, but helpers should only be introduced when the same assignments recur in matched functions.
Do not collapse apparently duplicated action-state functions if the original has separate functions; duplicate C bodies may be required for matching.
Scale/rotation reset callbacks can imply missing fighter variable types: a field passed to HSD_JObjSetScale is likely vector-shaped rather than raw u32 padding.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as fn_8012EC7C, fn_8012EDE8, fn_8012EFF4, fn_8013295C, and ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C.
New local helper names used descriptive subsystem/action style: ftYoshi_SpecialS_SetCall and fn_8012EC7C_inline.
Yoshi-specific fighter variables remain under fp->fv.ys and motion variables under fp->mv.ys.specials.
Unknown attribute fields are named by offset, such as x50, x5C, x7C, x8C, x90, xCC, and xE0, rather than guessed semantic names.
Float constants in touched code use uppercase F suffix, e.g. 0.0F.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(4) in functions where stack shape mattered.
Used an inline duplicate of fn_8012EC7C behavior to satisfy both standalone function matching and inlined call-site behavior in fn_8012EDE8.
Kept explicit motion IDs and flags such as 0x168, 0x169, 356, 360, 0x440012U, and 0x0C4C5092U instead of introducing enums not evidenced by the diff.
Used ternary absolute-value expressions for velocity magnitude checks instead of helper functions, likely to match generated code.
Preserved branch structure in fn_8012EFF4 with early returns around motion ID checks and velocity clamping.
Kept direct sign-flip assignments in fn_8013295C for self_vel.x, gr_vel, xE4_ground_accel_1, and several specials motion variables.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Only a brief positive comment was present; no technical review feedback or requested changes were captured in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_8012EC7C, fn_8012EDE8, fn_8012EFF4, fn_8013295C, or ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C to semantic Yoshi SpecialS names.
Search for later struct refinements to ftYoshiAttributes offsets x50, x5C, x7C, x8C, x90, xCC, and xE0.
Compare ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Phys and ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Phys against other fighter air-control physics functions for reusable matching patterns.
Check whether motion IDs 0x165, 0x167, 0x168, 0x169, 0x16A, and 0x16B were later replaced with named Yoshi motion enum constants.
Look for future cleanup of duplicated prototypes between ftYs_SpecialS.h and ftYs_SpecialHi.h.
